chunks.title: Traveling Salesman Problem - Branch and Bound - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: The topic is a traveling salesperson problem using branch and bound strategy.

chunks.title: Traveling Salesman Problem - Branch and Bound - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: In this video I will explain you about the problem then the approach of branch and bound

chunks.title: Traveling Salesman Problem - Branch and Bound - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: then I will solve a textbook problem using branch and bound.

chunks.title: Traveling Salesman Problem - Branch and Bound - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: See the problem is a weighted graph is given here is an example of 5 vertices graph and

chunks.title: Traveling Salesman Problem - Branch and Bound - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: we have to find out a shortest tour that is going through all the vertices once and returning

chunks.title: Traveling Salesman Problem - Branch and Bound - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: back to the starting vertex.

chunks.title: Traveling Salesman Problem - Branch and Bound - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So usually we take the starting vertex as 1 so we have to go around all the vertices

chunks.title: Traveling Salesman Problem - Branch and Bound - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: that is 2, 3, 4, 5 and again we have to come back to vertex 1.

chunks.title: Traveling Salesman Problem - Branch and Bound - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: For that a cause adjacency matrix is given here.

chunks.title: Traveling Salesman Problem - Branch and Bound - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now let us understand how this problem is solved using branch and bound.

chunks.title: Traveling Salesman Problem - Branch and Bound - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: For that I will take a small graph with just 4 vertices and I will discuss the approach

chunks.title: Traveling Salesman Problem - Branch and Bound - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: then we will solve this one.

chunks.title: Traveling Salesman Problem - Branch and Bound - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Suppose there is a graph with just 4 vertices and suppose the salesman is starting from

chunks.title: Traveling Salesman Problem - Branch and Bound - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: vertex 1 and he is going around all the vertices and returning back to vertex 1 and we want

chunks.title: Traveling Salesman Problem - Branch and Bound - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: the tour such that the cost is minimum.

chunks.title: Traveling Salesman Problem - Branch and Bound - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So he may be going 1, 2, 3, 4 then back to 1 or he may be going 1, 2, 3, 2, 4 then back

chunks.title: Traveling Salesman Problem - Branch and Bound - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: 1 he can go in any order such that the cost is minimized.

chunks.title: Traveling Salesman Problem - Branch and Bound - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So for exploring this problem we take the help of a state space tree and we say that

chunks.title: Traveling Salesman Problem - Branch and Bound - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: he is starting from vertex 1 and we start from 1 what are the vertices he can visit?

chunks.title: Traveling Salesman Problem - Branch and Bound - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: He can visit to 2 also, 3 also, 4 also so he can go to 2 also and 3 as well as he can

chunks.title: Traveling Salesman Problem - Branch and Bound - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: go on 4.

chunks.title: Traveling Salesman Problem - Branch and Bound - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: He can go on anyone but we want the one which is giving us minimum tour.

chunks.title: Traveling Salesman Problem - Branch and Bound - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: If he has reached on 2 then what are the remaining vertices?

chunks.title: Traveling Salesman Problem - Branch and Bound - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: He can visit vertex 3 also or 4 either this or this and from 3 he can go to 2 remaining

chunks.title: Traveling Salesman Problem - Branch and Bound - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: vertices then from 4 remaining vertices are 2 and 3 and when he has visited 1, 2 and 3

chunks.title: Traveling Salesman Problem - Branch and Bound - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: then he has to visit remaining vertex that is 4 only and here it is 3 and here it is

chunks.title: Traveling Salesman Problem - Branch and Bound - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: 4 and 2, 3 and 2.

chunks.title: Traveling Salesman Problem - Branch and Bound - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This is a state space tree that is exploring the entire problem showing all the possibilities

chunks.title: Traveling Salesman Problem - Branch and Bound - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: all the possible tours.

chunks.title: Traveling Salesman Problem - Branch and Bound - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Actually backtracking and branch and bound they are similar.

chunks.title: Traveling Salesman Problem - Branch and Bound - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: They both use state space tree but the approach for solving a problem is different.

chunks.title: Traveling Salesman Problem - Branch and Bound - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So let me explain the differences between backtracking and branch and bound here.

chunks.title: Traveling Salesman Problem - Branch and Bound - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: In backtracking first of all we will generate the state space tree but this is a complete

chunks.title: Traveling Salesman Problem - Branch and Bound - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: tree how it is generated?

chunks.title: Traveling Salesman Problem - Branch and Bound - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Let me show you the nodes in which order they are generated.

chunks.title: Traveling Salesman Problem - Branch and Bound - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: First this node then this one then this next this one then go back and this node and this

chunks.title: Traveling Salesman Problem - Branch and Bound - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: node then this node see just you follow my marker in this order they are generated.

chunks.title: Traveling Salesman Problem - Branch and Bound - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This is depth first search or like preorder and in backtracking if suppose there are multiple

chunks.title: Traveling Salesman Problem - Branch and Bound - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: solutions so actually for every end node it will give it as a solution but we don't want

chunks.title: Traveling Salesman Problem - Branch and Bound - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: all possible tours we want minimum cost tour.

chunks.title: Traveling Salesman Problem - Branch and Bound - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: I will give you an idea here if suppose you want a minimum cost tour find out this tour

chunks.title: Traveling Salesman Problem - Branch and Bound - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: find the cost, find this cost also but if suppose this cost is smaller than these then

chunks.title: Traveling Salesman Problem - Branch and Bound - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: take this one next find this cost also suppose this is becoming larger so discard this one

chunks.title: Traveling Salesman Problem - Branch and Bound - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: because we have a shorter path already then this is also larger discard it suppose this

chunks.title: Traveling Salesman Problem - Branch and Bound - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: is minimum then take that.

chunks.title: Traveling Salesman Problem - Branch and Bound - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Out of all possible answers you can track them and find out which is better and you

chunks.title: Traveling Salesman Problem - Branch and Bound - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: can take that one.

chunks.title: Traveling Salesman Problem - Branch and Bound - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: You can use backtracking also for solving the problems but backtracking is much time

chunks.title: Traveling Salesman Problem - Branch and Bound - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: consuming that is the reason for solving optimization that is minimization or maximization problems

chunks.title: Traveling Salesman Problem - Branch and Bound - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: we avoid backtracking we use it for permutation problems.

chunks.title: Traveling Salesman Problem - Branch and Bound - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now coming to branch and bound see the same problem when it is solved using branch and

chunks.title: Traveling Salesman Problem - Branch and Bound - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: bound we use level order.

chunks.title: Traveling Salesman Problem - Branch and Bound - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Watch here the order in which the nodes are generated this one second then this one then

chunks.title: Traveling Salesman Problem - Branch and Bound - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: this one then this this so in this order the nodes are generated and while generating the

chunks.title: Traveling Salesman Problem - Branch and Bound - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: nodes for every node we find out the cost and if for any node if you are getting the

chunks.title: Traveling Salesman Problem - Branch and Bound - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: cost greater than some upper bound then we will kill that node.

chunks.title: Traveling Salesman Problem - Branch and Bound - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So we will try to produce or generate only those nodes which are fruitful we will not

chunks.title: Traveling Salesman Problem - Branch and Bound - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: generate all the nodes we will try to follow the path which is taking us nearer to the

chunks.title: Traveling Salesman Problem - Branch and Bound - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: minimized or maximized optimized solution node.

chunks.title: Traveling Salesman Problem - Branch and Bound - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So that's how branch and bound is different from backtracking.

chunks.title: Traveling Salesman Problem - Branch and Bound - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now let me solve this problem using branch and bound.

text: The topic is a traveling salesperson problem using branch and bound strategy. In this video I will explain you about the problem then the approach of branch and bound then I will solve a textbook problem using branch and bound. See the problem is a weighted graph is given here is an example of 5 vertices graph and we have to find out a shortest tour that is going through all the vertices once and returning back to the starting vertex. So usually we take the starting vertex as 1 so we have to go around all the vertices that is 2, 3, 4, 5 and again we have to come back to vertex 1. For that a cause adjacency matrix is given here. Now let us understand how this problem is solved using branch and bound. For that I will take a small graph with just 4 vertices and I will discuss the approach then we will solve this one. Suppose there is a graph with just 4 vertices and suppose the salesman is starting from vertex 1 and he is going around all the vertices and returning back to vertex 1 and we want the tour such that the cost is minimum. So he may be going 1, 2, 3, 4 then back to 1 or he may be going 1, 2, 3, 2, 4 then back 1 he can go in any order such that the cost is minimized. So for exploring this problem we take the help of a state space tree and we say that he is starting from vertex 1 and we start from 1 what are the vertices he can visit? He can visit to 2 also, 3 also, 4 also so he can go to 2 also and 3 as well as he can go on 4. He can go on anyone but we want the one which is giving us minimum tour. If he has reached on 2 then what are the remaining vertices? He can visit vertex 3 also or 4 either this or this and from 3 he can go to 2 remaining vertices then from 4 remaining vertices are 2 and 3 and when he has visited 1, 2 and 3 then he has to visit remaining vertex that is 4 only and here it is 3 and here it is 4 and 2, 3 and 2. This is a state space tree that is exploring the entire problem showing all the possibilities all the possible tours. Actually backtracking and branch and bound they are similar. They both use state space tree but the approach for solving a problem is different. So let me explain the differences between backtracking and branch and bound here. In backtracking first of all we will generate the state space tree but this is a complete tree how it is generated? Let me show you the nodes in which order they are generated. First this node then this one then this next this one then go back and this node and this node then this node see just you follow my marker in this order they are generated. This is depth first search or like preorder and in backtracking if suppose there are multiple solutions so actually for every end node it will give it as a solution but we don't want all possible tours we want minimum cost tour. I will give you an idea here if suppose you want a minimum cost tour find out this tour find the cost, find this cost also but if suppose this cost is smaller than these then take this one next find this cost also suppose this is becoming larger so discard this one because we have a shorter path already then this is also larger discard it suppose this is minimum then take that. Out of all possible answers you can track them and find out which is better and you can take that one. You can use backtracking also for solving the problems but backtracking is much time consuming that is the reason for solving optimization that is minimization or maximization problems we avoid backtracking we use it for permutation problems. Now coming to branch and bound see the same problem when it is solved using branch and bound we use level order. Watch here the order in which the nodes are generated this one second then this one then this one then this this so in this order the nodes are generated and while generating the nodes for every node we find out the cost and if for any node if you are getting the cost greater than some upper bound then we will kill that node. So we will try to produce or generate only those nodes which are fruitful we will not generate all the nodes we will try to follow the path which is taking us nearer to the minimized or maximized optimized solution node. So that's how branch and bound is different from backtracking. Now let me solve this problem using branch and bound. So the problem is solved by just using matrices let us start and see what we have to do first. The very first thing what we have to do is take the given matrix that is cost adjacency matrix for a given graph I have taken it here reduce this matrix what does it mean by reducing a matrix?
